name: Italian Vegetable and Potato Stew
recipeIngredient: 1 large Eggplant, peeled and cut into 1-inch cubes
3 tablespoons Olive oil or more as needed
1 Green bell pepper, seeded and chopped
3 medium Boiling potatoes, cut into 1/2-inch thick slices
1 can Unsweetened tomatoes in puree (28 oz)
1/2 cup Fresh basil, chopped OR 1 tablespoon Dried basil
1 cup Mediterranean black olives, pitted and chopped
recipeInstructions:
1. Place the eggplant in a colander and toss with 1 teaspoon of the salt. Let stand for 1 hour to draw out the bitter juices.
2. Rinse well, drain, and pat dry with kitchen towels.
3. Heat 2 tablespoons of the oil in a large saucepan over medium heat. Add the eggplant and cook, stirring often, until lightly browned, about 4 minutes. (You may have to add a little more oil if the eggplant sticks).
4. Transfer the eggplant to a plate and set aside.
5. Heat the remaining 2 tablespoons of oil in the pan, and add the onion, celery, bell pepper, and garlic. Cook, stirring often, until softened, about 5 minutes.
6. Stir in the reserved eggplant, potatoes, tomatoes with their puree, vegetable stock, the remaining 1/4 teaspoon salt, and red pepper flakes; if using dried basil, add it now.
7. Bring to a simmer, then reduce the heat to low, cover, and simmer, stirring occasionally, until the potatoes are tender, about 45 minutes to 1 hour.
8. Stir in the olives and fresh basil (if using), and continue cooking to blend the flavors, about 5 minutes.
9. Serve the stew hot, warm, or at room temperature.
